sections.paragraph:
στυγνός, ή, όν gloomy, sad (so since Aeschyl.; X., An. 2, 6, 9. Also PSI 28, 1 στυγνοῦ σκότους; LXX; TestLevi 3:1; Jos., Ant. 19, 318) Hv 1, 2, 3 opp. γελῶν as GJs 17:2. τί ‹οὕ›τως στυγνὸς καὶ κατηφής, κύριε; Why so sad and downcast, Lord? AcPl Ha 7, 30.—DELG s.v. στύγεω.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hv Hv = Visions

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 949
